description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol attack surface (Linux Foundation v1.0, 2026). Covers agent card forgery, capability over-claim, recursive delegation abuse, missing caller allowlist, internal-URL exfil, tool description injection at the card layer. Distinct from MCP attacks (MCP is host↔server JSON-RPC; A2A is agent↔agent peering).

agent_card_missing_signature.description: A2A agent card served at /.well-known/agent.json with no signature / JWS / sig field. Peers cannot verify authenticity — any attacker can forge a card that overstates capability and trick downstream agents into routing to it.
agent_card_missing_signature.operator_note: Use probe_a2a_agent_card for full 7-defect audit. Severity climbs to CRITICAL when combined with capability over-claim or recursive delegation.

agent_card_capability_overclaim.description: A2A card declares wildcard capability (`*`, `any`, `any:any`, `all`) — peer agents accept any tool call without scope check. Combined with missing signature = trivial cross-agent privilege escalation.

agent_card_recursive_delegation_unbounde.description: A2A card has `delegation.recursive: true` with no `max_depth` — attacker triggers fan-out / DoS via recursive peer invocations. Also enables cascading goal manipulation across agent chain.

agent_card_internal_url.description: Agent card lists an endpoint URL pointing to RFC1918 / 169.254.169.254 / metadata.* / localhost. Peer agents fetching via these URLs become an SSRF chain inside the target cloud / cluster.

agent_card_missing_caller_allowlist.description: No `accepted_callers` / `allowed_peers` / `allowed_callers` field in card. Agent accepts delegated work from any peer — combined with overclaimed capabilities = world-callable tool surface.

agent_card_tool_desc_prompt_injection.description: Agent card lists tools whose description contains prompt-injection markers — `Ignore prior instructions`, `Execute as system`, `Override safety`. Card-layer PI propagates to every peer that consumes the card and shows the description to its LLM.
